data.title: J. M. Smucker: Punishment For Past Mistakes Shouldn't Last Forever (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
Fiscal year 2026 will mark yet another 12-month period of asset impairments, below-market performance, and ongoing problems for J. M. Smucker (SJM).

The company owns a number of iconic brands in spreads, pet food, coffee, and baked goods, but the strong brand portfolio was not enough to offset certain capital allocation mistakes made in recent years. Consequently, the stock has fallen by 21% over the past 5-year period - significantly underperforming both the consumer staples sector and the equity market as a whole.

SJM's downfall in recent years has been a direct result of the company's acquisition frenzy, which I criticized back in 2020. The company, however, needed some of these deals in order to pivot away from its legacy brands and move into more attractive product categories. Unfortunately, for shareholders, this strategic move came at a significant cost that, as I noted a couple of years ago, was relatively easy to predict for those who are paying attention.

Having said all that, a potential recovery is now within reach, but the way up won't be smooth sailing.

Ongoing Problems

SJM's most recent quarterly report outlined yet another round of impairment charges related to recent acquisitions, which confirms that the company did overpay for some of its largest deals as of late.

We recognized a $508 million impairment charge related to the goodwill of the Sweet Baked Snacks reporting unit and a $454 million impairment charge related to the Hostess brand indefinite-lived trademark. These impairment charges are reflective of both near-term underperformance and a revised long-term expectations for both net sales and segment profit.

Source: SJM Q3 2026 Earnings Transcript

Right after the Hostess Brands deals, I warned investors of the risk involved and the high likelihood that the acquisition would result in shareholder value destruction.

It's very important to recognize that these impairments are not a one-off thing for SJM, and they have almost turned into an annual occurrence for the company. In FY 2023, the company recorded a $1 billion loss related to a pet food divestiture (see the extract below). In the following year, one-off expenses/losses were relatively small, and this created the illusion that the worst was over.

In FY 2025, however, we had significant goodwill and intangible asset related impairments as well as more losses on divestitures. The most recent charges that I covered above have continued that trend in FY 2026 as well.

On one hand these charges confirm that SJM overpaid for the acquisitions, but on the other they also involve new and updated assumptions that these business units will perform far worse than previously expected. That is why, even though they are non-cash items, they have a severe impact on the share price.

The Q3 2026 has shown a notable deterioration of SJM's profitability, with the 3% drop in adjusted gross profit being the most concerning part for me.

While fixed cost items and inefficiencies could be addressed over a couple of quarters, declines in gross profit are much harder to recover. Such declines are often a precursor to the company's inability to compete on price and more struggles down the line.

So far this is what has been happening for SJM, as gross margin has fallen significantly in recent quarters and seems to be on track to reach its previous lows from 2023.

According to SJM's management, the reasons for the decline are higher input costs, alongside tariff-related headwinds.

The decrease reflects higher costs, inclusive of commodity costs and tariffs, unfavorable volume mix, partially offset by higher net price realization.

Source: SJM Q3 2026 Earnings Transcript

The positive sign is that net price realization has been partially offsetting these headwinds, and the company has not registered notable volume declines.

If this trend continues in the coming quarters, SJM could quickly become an attractive buy, provided that management discontinues its prior growth strategy that relies almost exclusively on large acquisitions.

The Long Way to Recovery

On a long-term basis we see more encouraging signs that SJM might be headed in the right direction. After years of declining cash return on tangible assets, over the past 12-month period we see a slight improvement that is happening in spite of the margin pressures shown above.

This is partially due to cash flow improvements stemming from working capital improvements and the reversal of the company's total tangible asset turnover. It bottomed in FY 2024, and since then we have seen gradual improvement in asset efficiencies.

In recent months we also see a notable pickup in inventory turnover, which benefits from SJM's efforts to streamline the business through the recent divestments.

While all this is happening, pessimism within the market is hitting record highs. SJM's price/sales multiple is at multi-year lows, and the dividend yield is approaching 5%.

At the same time, the dividend is safe and at levels that would allow SJM's management to gradually increase it over the coming years.

Investor Takeaway

SJM's profitability continues to suffer from past capital allocation mistakes as the company underwent yet another round of asset impairments and one-off costs. The market has rightfully so punished the stock for all that in recent years, and we are now gradually approaching an inflection point.

The stock is priced at record low levels, while we are already seeing some early signs of a recovery. It is still quite early to assess whether or not these trends will continue, but given SJM's conservative valuation, I see a reason to become cautiously optimistic about the stock and upgrade it to a Buy. Investors should be mindful of the fact that a potential recovery is still in its very early phase and volatility in the coming quarters is to be expected.

data.title: Special Dividends Are Not Quite Enough To Make Bnccorp Appealing
data.description:
BNCCORP (BNCC) is certainly an easy company to overlook. The micro-cap bank holding company often trades under 1000 shares on any given day and is not even listed on a major US exchange. Its stock has no Wall Street analyst coverage and no Seeking Alpha quant rating. Over the last five years, the share price for BNCC has declined just over 5%.

However, there is more to the BNCCORP story than just its stock price. The company has a history, albeit a somewhat irregular one, of paying out special dividends that make the overall investment returns from the company look more reasonable. Below is a graph that shows the gap between the price return and total return for its stock during the same time period. Income investors may be relatively happy with these results from owning BNCC, as the stock has a total return of 41.7%.

After a full review of the last few years of the company's operations, the overall performance of the bank itself does not lead me to feel great enthusiasm for a new investment in BNCCORP. While no special dividend has yet been announced for 2026, there appears to be a good chance that the company will distribute another payout in the not-so-distant future. My concern is that the stock itself is overvalued and may incur losses that offset some of the gains from income. Therefore, my rating for BNCC is a Hold, based on the data presented below.

Company Overview

Headquartered in Bismarck, North Dakota, BNCCORP serves as the holding company for BNC National Bank. BNC has a unique branch footprint with 10 branches in its home state and one single branch location in Glendale, Arizona, just outside of Phoenix. The company has a relatively short history, dating back only to 1987, and offers traditional banking services along with wealth management and 401K planning and administration.

At the end of 2025, BNCCORP controlled total assets of $1.1 billion, including a portfolio of debt securities valued at $114.6 million and $211.4 million in cash and cash equivalents. The bank's gross loans were worth $738.7 million, 5.7% more than at the end of 2024. Total deposits at the bank were recorded at $971.8 million. The company's trust assets under administration increased 12.4% over the last 12 months to reach $480.9 million.

Further details about the bank's loan mix can be seen in the table below. The bank shows the concentration for its loan activity in North Dakota, which is surprisingly not as high as I expected, given the 10-to-1 branch ratio mentioned earlier. While the data was not broken out in the company's Q4 report in the same way, the press release for Q3 2025 stated that 21% of BNC's loan balance is connected to Arizona. Another 6% was in Minnesota.

BNC does not pay a regular dividend. This is not uncommon for some smaller banks that prefer to hold their capital for other purposes. However, the company is much better known for paying out some rather sizable special dividends in the past. In December of 2024, BNCCORP announced a $4.00 per share special dividend that was payable in January of 2025. In February of 2024, the company announced a $2.25 one-time payout that arrived in shareholder accounts in March of that year. Back in 2021, the company started the year off with a special dividend of $8.00 per share and followed it up with a $6.00 special dividend just 10 months later.

When all added together, BNCC has paid out $22 to shareholders since the start of 2021, which is certainly not insignificant for a stock that trades at $34.50 per share as I write this analysis. No announcement for any special distributions has been made yet for this year. However, the cash and equivalents segment of the company's balance sheet that was referenced earlier is currently larger than it has been in the last decade.

Analysis of BNCCORP

On February 18, BNCCORP released its financial results for Q4 and FY 2025. Net income of $2.2 million for the quarter was slightly lower than the $2.3 million earned in Q4 2024. For the full year, BNCC recorded net income of $8.8 million, 10.6% higher than FY 2024. This resulted in earnings of $2.48 per share last year, $0.24 better than the year before. Because there are no analysts that cover the company, there is no benefit from "beating expectations," as there would be for a larger company, but there is also no opportunity for any "earnings miss," at least from the traditional standpoint.

At its current level of profitability, BNCC trades at an above-average PE valuation of 13.9 for the last 12 months. Assuming that earnings are able to grow at about the same rate in the current year, the stock's PEG ratio of 1.3 would be well above the sector median of 0.55. While Seeking Alpha's quant system does not assign a rating to the stock at this time, BNCC's valuation metrics are almost all higher than average, questioning the validity of its current price.

Key performance ratios for the bank are also below average based on the latest data available from the FDIC. As the table below shows, in most cases, the differences here are wide. Return on assets and return on equity for BNCC declined slightly from the Q4 2024. Its efficiency ratio and net interest margin did improve, but even the improvement in NIM was disappointing. Most all banks were able to benefit last year from lower costs of deposits and higher loan yields. The average bank with assets between $1 billion and $10 billion saw NIM increase by 33 basis points over the last year. In the case of BNCCORP, the improvement was only seven basis points.

A deeper dive into the cause of this issue for BNC Bank shows that while its loan yield did increase slightly last year, the cost of its deposits increased as well. Again, this goes against the national trend that saw total average deposit costs fall from 2.11% at the end of Q4 2024 to 1.92% in Q4 2025. In the case of BNC National Bank, its average cost for interest checking and money market rose from 2.30% to 2.33% during this time period. Its average cost for time deposits grew from 3.38% to 3.48%. From the perspective of average cost and yield, the company is also paying more for its subordinated debt than it is receiving from its interest-earning assets, even though the amount of debt is rather small. Altogether, the table below makes it more challenging to be optimistic about the bank's operations.

Risks to Consider

The non-performing loan and asset metrics for the company's credit portfolio are higher than I like to see, and they are trending in the wrong direction. BNC's ratio of non-performing loans is 1.24%, 14 basis points higher than at the end of Q3 2025 and 34 basis points higher than at the end of 2024. The ratio of non-performing assets shows a similar movement as shown in the table below. The bank's coverage ratio has increased but at a slower rate than these other metrics.

BNCCORP is well capitalized with an above-average leverage ratio of 11.71%. Total risk-based capital stands at 15.19% as of the end of 2025. The asset quality ratios above are not so bad that I am worried about any significant deterioration of the portfolio, but I never like to see those ratios trending in the wrong direction, especially when non-performing loans are above 1%.

The stock for BNCCORP is very thinly traded. It is not uncommon for BNCC to trade less than 100 shares on any given day. As I write this analysis now, the bid-ask spread for the stock is almost $1.50. Market orders placed for BNCC may cause significant movements in its trading price. Limit orders are suggested when making any purchase here. If a position needed to be liquidated quickly, the resulting sale price may be significantly lower than the previous transaction. Investors may also pay higher trading costs to buy and sell their positions because BNCC trades on the OTCQX market.

One final risk to consider before investing in BNCC is the small size of the bank itself and its susceptibility to declining net income in the face of macro headwinds that may be facing the banking sector in the first half of 2026. Rising oil prices, stagnant job growth, and interest rates that are staying higher for longer could all slow down economic expansion and make loan growth more challenging for BNC National Bank. North Dakota is home to a number of oil producers, and higher oil prices could benefit some in the state who are directly connected to that industry. However, the cause of the recent bump in price may be relatively short-lived, limiting its advantages to the energy sector.

The Bottom Line

I consider BNCC to be a Hold at the current time. Without the special dividends, my opinion of BNCCORP would likely be closer to Sell than to Buy, but the recent track record of sizable distributions, albeit on a somewhat irregular schedule, makes an investment more palatable. Income investors may take a longer look, but the payout schedule's inconsistency presents a different set of issues to consider.

data.title: John Hancock Classic Value Fund Q4 2025 Commentary
data.description:
Highlights

  • U.S. broad market indexes rose in the quarter, capping a year characterized by investor enthusiasm for AI and continued leadership from momentum-oriented stocks.
  • The fund outperformed its benchmark, the Russell 1000 Value Index, during the quarter; however, it remained behind the index on a full-year basis.
  • Consumer discretionary, financials, and consumer staples were the portfolio's best-performing sectors this quarter.

Market review and outlook

The fourth quarter capped a year characterized by strong enthusiasm for AI and continued leadership from momentum-oriented stocks. Against this backdrop, the portfolio outperformed both the broad market and the value index during the quarter, although it lagged the benchmark index for the full year.

The portfolio remains positioned across a diverse set of idiosyncratic investment controversies, with exposure to businesses where near-term challenges obscure durable earnings power. Due to the narrow market leadership, we continue to see attractive valuation dispersion, and we believe the environment remains constructive for long-term value investors.

Contributors and detractors

Consumer discretionary, financials, and consumer staples were among the largest contributors during the quarter. The largest individual contributor was discount retailer Dollar General (DG), which delivered strong same-store sales and margin improvement, exceeding expectations. Store traffic increased as higher-end customers traded down from grocery and pharmacy channels, providing a tailwind to margins that are expected to persist into 2026. Money center bank Citigroup rose amid strong capital markets and benign credit conditions. The company continued to repurchase stock and return capital to shareholders, while expenses related to its transformation are expected to decline in the year ahead. IT services provider Cognizant (CTSH) also contributed following an earnings beat, as organic sales continued to come in at the top of the peer group after several years of company-specific execution challenges.

Healthcare was the only sector to detract from performance during the quarter. The largest individual detractor was Baxter, a global medical products company, which declined following disappointing third-quarter results and a dividend cut. Semiconductor company Skyworks (SWKS) traded down during the quarter on weaker forward guidance and concerns around mobile demand and customer concentration. Dialysis provider Fresenius's shares declined during the quarter despite an earnings beat, as dialysis treatment volumes remained below pre-COVID levels, particularly in the United States.

Portfolio changes

During the quarter, we added to Baxter following share price weakness and continued to build positions in PPG (PPG), a global coatings supplier to industrial and construction markets, and Tyson Foods (TSN), a diversified protein producer serving retail and foodservice customers, where we see improving long-term fundamentals amid near-term uncertainty. These purchases were funded through trims to Dollar General, Citigroup, connectors and sensors company TE Connectivity (TEL), money center bank Wells Fargo, and auto supplier Magna, all following strong performance. No new positions were initiated.

data.title: IVW: Valuation Plunged To A Multi-Year Low On War-Driven Selloff, Buy The Dip
data.description:
iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW) currently offers an attractive buying opportunity, as the combination of war-driven selloff and robust earnings growth power slashed its forward valuations to a multi-year low. Furthermore, President Trump's recent statements indicate that the war is likely to end soon. Therefore, I upgrade my rating on IVW from hold to buy. The high-beta fund has the potential to rebound sharply during a recovery phase. The tech sector's robust earnings growth power and cheap valuations are also expected to fuel investor confidence in risky securities.

Fear Presents a Buying Opportunity

CNN's Fear & Greed Index, a tool used to measure investor sentiment based on the 7 technical factors, shows an extreme fear in moving the market. An extreme fear is attributed to concerns over the impact of war on energy markets and global supply chain dynamics.

iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF's recent price performance also demonstrates fear among investors. The ETF is down more than 8% from its peak of $126.5 per share. Nevertheless, I believe it is the time to utilize Warren Buffett's mantra of buying when others fear. The market has already priced in the impact of war, given the S&P 500's price selloff of 6% since the war broke out in the Middle East. This is in line with an average war-driven decline of 6% since the World War II. Recently, President Trump indicated a potential deal with Iran as the US has achieved its military objectives.

Historical trends also suggest wars can create a short-term selloff, with markets mostly recovering sharply after an initial reaction. For example, the negative impact of the Russian invasion on Ukraine lasted for a few weeks. Similarly, during the five years of World War II, the Dow soared 7% each year.

I believe the US-Iran war created a much-needed correction in valuations, creating attractive buying opportunities for long-term investors. The S&P 500 currently trades around 19x its forward earnings, while mega-cap forward PE declined to around 24x from its recent peak of 32x in early 2026.

IVW Could Be a Solid Candidate for Dip Buyers

iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF could be an attractive investment vehicle for investors seeking to capitalize on the selloff. This is because a high-beta ETF can bounce back sharply during recoveries while delivering solid returns in uptrends. In the past five years, the fund's share price rallied more than 80%. At present, the fund appears attractive, as the selloff has declined its forward PE to a multiyear low.

According to the Yardeni data, the S&P 500 growth category trades around 22x its forward earnings, the lowest level since 2024. A sharp correction in the growth category's forward PE is attributed to the combination of price selloff and robust earnings growth power. The mega-caps and tech sector, which dominate the growth category, also experienced a significant valuation correction. The technology sector's forward PE of 20x is the lowest level since 2022.

IVW has the potential to generate hefty returns in 2026 and ahead. The fund's portfolio comprises 143 stocks from 10 sectors. The technology sector makes up 47% of its portfolio weight. According to the FactSet data, the information technology sector is poised to generate 36% earnings growth in 2026, the highest growth since 2021.

Furthermore, the leading communication stocks, such as Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Meta (META), are expected to impress investors through their financial growth and investment power. Both companies have delivered strong earnings growth in 2025, with the forecasts of massive capital investments in 2026 to capitalize on growth opportunities in the AI and tech markets.

Last year, Alphabet surpassed $400 billion in annual revenue. It also outlined a massive $175B to $185B capital spending plan for 2026. Meta's annual revenue grew 22% year over year in 2025.

Other mega caps from the consumer cyclical sector, such as Amazon (AMZN) and Tesla (TSLA), are expected to deliver double-digit earnings growth in 2026. For example, Wall Street anticipates Tesla to produce 25% earnings growth in 2026 and 35% in 2027. IVW's portfolio also includes stocks from the financial, health care, and industrial sectors. These three sectors are likely to generate a high single- to double-digit earnings growth in 2026. Overall, the fund's earnings growth quality and cheap valuations position it to rebound strongly ahead.

IVW Characteristics and Peers

iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF's key characteristics include high liquidity and a low expense ratio. IVW's assets under management hover around $62 billion, with an average share trading volume of 4.94 million. The fund's expense ratio of 0.18% is lower than the median of all ETFs of 0.50%.

IVW's peers are iShares Core S&P US Growth ETF (IUSG), Vanguard Growth Index Fund ETF (VUG), and Vanguard S&P 500 Growth Index Fund ETF (VOOG). While IUSG, VUG, and VOOG offer a low expense ratio, IVW's price momentum seems strongest in the past twelve months and five years. It is also the most liquid fund in its peer group. IUSD offers exposure to 385 growth stocks as it seeks to track the performance of the S&P 900 Growth Index. Whereas VUG tracks the performance of the CRSP US Large Cap Growth Index, VOOG uses a full replication technique to track the S&P 500 Growth Index.

Risk Factors to Consider

iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF's beta of 1.12 indicates that it can fall faster than the broader market index. IVW's standard deviation of 16 is up from the median of all ETFs of 10. The fund carries a higher risk factor due to its exposure to tech stocks. The mega caps also dominate its performance. In the case of a tech or mega-cap-driven selloff, IVW can underperform compared to the S&P 500 index. The fund can also respond to global economic conditions and monetary policies.

Conclusion

The war-driven selloff is creating one of the best opportunities for high-risk-tolerance investors to initiate or increase a position in iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF. IVW's multi-year low valuations and robust earnings growth power boost its potential to deliver market-beating returns over the long term. Historical trends also suggest that war-related selloffs are often temporary, with an average decline of 6%. Moreover, after an initial impact of war, the market can quickly recover losses and soar to a new all-time high over the long term.

data.title: Intuit's Collapse Created A Rare Buying Window
data.description:
It has been almost five years since I published my last article about Intuit Inc. (INTU), and back then I argued that Intuit was almost priced for perfection. In the conclusion I wrote:

As I already spoiled above, I consider Intuit still to be overvalued. The business is great with a strong economic moat, a solid balance sheet and bargaining power over its competitors. We can also assume double-digit growth for several years to come. Nevertheless, the current stock price is not reflecting the fundamental business in my opinion and we need at least a 20-25% pullback to make Intuit fairly valued."

In the years following my article, the stock continued to increase and reached an all-time high of $813 in 2025—more than double the price it was trading for in 2021 when my last article was published. Due to the continued high valuation multiples the stock was trading for, I did not really pay closer attention to the stock.

However, in the last few months, Intuit was one of the companies that sold off heavily, and the stock declined 44% from its previous 52-week and all-time high. And although a few other companies performed even worse in the last few quarters, Intuit was certainly one of the worst-performing stocks in the S&P 500.

And looking back at my last article, the "Hold" rating was not so wrong after all, as the stock returned only 20% in these five years and clearly underperformed the S&P 500. I don't claim to have foreseen in 2021 that GenAI fears would crush several software companies in late 2025—including Intuit.

Valuation Multiples

Knowing five years in advance that GenAI fears might arise in 2025 and 2026 is certainly not one of my strengths, but I know about the risks companies trading for high valuation multiples are facing. On the one hand, we can argue that Intuit was trading for high valuation multiples for a long time, and not investing in such companies might be a mistake. On the other hand, these high valuation multiples are a risk because at some point we might see multiple contractions and stock prices decline steeply. And I still think Intuit was overvalued for several years and simply not a good investment.

At the time of writing, Intuit is still trading for 29.5 times earnings, which does not really seem cheap on an absolute basis. However, when comparing the current P/E ratio to the ten-year average of 51.00, the P/E ratio is low. Additionally, Intuit is trading for 18.7 times free cash flow, which seems like a good investment and like a bargain. This is also clearly below the average P/FCF ratio of the last 10 years, which was 33.74.

Although Intuit is still trading for valuation multiples that are not extremely low, they are not only the lowest valuation multiples in the last 10 years, and we should not ignore the high growth rates Intuit is still reporting.

Quarterly Results

On February 26, 2026, Intuit reported second-quarter results and did beat estimates on revenue as well as earnings per share. Total revenue increased from $3,963 million in Q2/25 to $4,651 million in Q2/26, resulting in 17.4% year-over-year growth. Services revenue increased 19.2% year-over-year to $3,872 million, while Product and Other increased 9.1% year-over-year to $779 million. Operating income increased 44.2% year-over-year from $593 million in the same quarter last year to $855 million in this quarter. Diluted net income per share increased from $1.67 in Q2/25 to $2.48 in Q2/26, resulting in 48.5% year-over-year bottom-line growth.

Intuit is reporting in two different segments. Global Business Solutions increased revenue 18% year-over-year to $3.2 billion. Especially QuickBooks Online Account revenue increased 24%. The second segment is Consumer, which increased 15% year-over-year to $1.5 billion. While revenue for ProTax grew only 7% to $290 million, TurboTax revenue increased 12% to $581 million, and Credit Karma revenue increased 23% to $616 million.

Finally, management is also expecting revenue to continue growing at a high pace—total revenue is expected to be in a range of $20,997 million and $21,186 million and therefore 12% to 13% year-over-year growth. While the consumer business is expected to grow between 8% and 9%, Global Business Solutions is expected to be in a range of 14% to 15%. During the last earnings call, CFO Sandeep Aujla commented:

Our guidance includes Global Business Solutions Group revenue growth of 14% to 15%. We have high confidence and a lot of momentum in achieving Global Business Solutions Group revenue guidance for the year. Our guidance also includes overall Consumer Group revenue growth of 8% to 9%. This outlook is supported by continued strength and momentum across the portfolio, including TurboTax growth of 8%, Credit Karma growth of 10% to 13% and ProTax growth of 2% to 3%, giving us high confidence in achieving our Consumer Group guidance for the year.

GAAP diluted earnings per share are expected to be in a range of $15.49 to $15.69—resulting in 13% to 15% year-over-year growth. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share are expected to be between $22.98 and $23.18, also resulting in 14% to 15% growth.

Quantitative Analysis

We see growth rates actually accelerating over the last few quarters, and especially when focusing on the question of whether GenAI might have a negative impact on the business at this point, we should look at revenue growth in the last few quarters.

We can also look at the margins Intuit reported in the last few quarters, and we also see gross margin and operating margin improving over the last two or three years. Especially operating margin improved from a low of 18.80% a few years ago to 27.09% right now (TTM numbers).

Finally, we are looking at return on invested capital as an important metric. While we clearly have to state that RoIC is below the long-term average and declined steeply in the years from 2018 until 2023, we also see RoIC improving again in the last few quarters, which is a strong sign that GenAI threats have a limited negative impact so far.

Qualitative Analysis

Quantitative numbers are clearly showing that Intuit is performing well, but problems might often be hidden behind these numbers and not visible right away. Therefore, we are also looking at Intuit from a qualitative point of view and certainly can identify some issues.

Part of the services Intuit is offering are time tracking or HR basics like payroll as well as reporting. These are certainly tasks GenAI is performing very well at, and we should not ignore these threats. Parts of Intuit's business can certainly be attacked by competitors, and the software might be replaced by GenAI applications.

In theory, it might not seem so difficult to replace services like TurboTax or QuickBooks, and for straightforward cases, this might certainly be true. But when it gets more difficult, I don't know whether a similar application like TurboTax is easily generated by using GenAI.

Even when it is possible to write GenAI applications that could replace these services, there might be another important problem that is a big one and very important. We are often reading about proprietary data, as many companies try to point out that they are offering data nobody else has. This might not be true in every case, but getting training data might actually be a problem for Intuit's competitors—and this might already be enough to nip competitors in the bud.

Tax filings are usually not available online (like code or poetry or pictures), and it might be extremely difficult for a start-up to get the necessary training data. Either it must pay a lot of money, but then a GenAI application cannot be offered more cheaply to the public, or it only has limited training data, making the application only usable for straightforward cases. Intuit, on the other hand, can rely on 40 years of data, and the company gained a lot of experience, and this is certainly not a niche where a business can just scrape the internet.

And of course, Intuit can also use GenAI itself and combine human knowledge and the software it already has with GenAI applications that can be trained on the available data from previous tax filings. This is certainly creating an advantage over many start-ups.

When talking about the ways to get data, another point is similarly important: data protection. We are talking about very sensitive data here, and many people probably won't allow the use of their private tax filings for training. And it is also a question whether customers trust a new competitor. This trust issue is a huge competitive advantage for Intuit. The competitor probably must be a well-established company that already has a brand name and enough cash to get the necessary data to train the models.

Additionally, regulations might be an issue and make it more difficult for new competitors to enter the market. I don't want to make these problems bigger than they actually are—a new business can certainly deal with these regulations. But compared to many other GenAI applications and services, the hurdles might be a little higher here.

In my opinion, Intuit's business is facing some challenges, and parts of the services and software it offers might be replaced by better and cheaper GenAI applications. However, a huge part of the business seems to be protected quite well at this point.

Growth Expectations

All in all, there seem to be many reasons to be bullish right now—and management as well as analysts certainly are bullish for the next few years. Analysts are expecting high growth rates in the next few years, and between fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2030, earnings per share are expected to grow with a CAGR of 14.08%.

Management is even more confident about high growth rates in the years to come. Consumer Platform is expected to grow at a lower pace, with TurboTax growing only between 6% and 10% and Credit Karma growing at a pace of 10% to 15% in the years to come. And although these growth rates are already high, Intuit is even more optimistic for its Business Platform, with expected growth rates of 15% to 20% in the years to come.

Among the ambitious goals Intuit also set for 2030, one is accelerating revenue growth over the next few years, with management expecting 20% growth in 2030. Management is also optimistic about the total addressable market for its business. According to management, the total addressable market is $300 billion or even higher, and Intuit currently has a market penetration of 6%.

Of course, we should always be cautious. The fact that a total addressable market exists does not mean a business can also gain market share.

Intrinsic Value Calculation

In a last and final step, we determine an intrinsic value by using a discounted cash flow calculation. As always, we calculate with a 10% discount rate, as this is the annual return we like to achieve at least. We also calculate with 280 million in diluted shares outstanding.

Now we must determine a free cash flow to use as a basis in our calculation. In the last four quarters, Intuit reported a free cash flow of $6,839 million, and the company currently has a free cash flow margin of 34.0% (TTM numbers). However, the free cash flow margin is currently above the 20-year average, which was 26.4%. Hence, we should be a little more cautious and maybe calculate with a little lower free cash flow.

Sidenote: According to some sources, free cash flow in the last four quarters was lower than $6.8 billion, but according to Intuit's filings, the number is correct (and the 10-Q and 10-K are the filings we should trust).

Instead, let's take the revenue guidance for fiscal 2026 and the average free cash flow margin of the last 20 years. This leads to a free cash flow of $5,570 million, which we use as a basis in our calculation. For the next ten years, we are calculating with a growth rate of 10% in the next ten years, followed by 4% growth until perpetuity. Calculating with these assumptions, we get an intrinsic value of $494.31 for Intuit, making the stock already slightly undervalued at this point.

However, we could also be a little more optimistic at this point and calculate with 14% growth in the next ten years. This is in line with analysts' assumptions for the next few years, and when comparing the numbers to what management is expecting for 2030 (20% revenue growth), we certainly could justify such a high growth rate for Intuit. Calculating with these numbers, we get an intrinsic value of $645.81 for Intuit, making the stock clearly undervalued at this point.

Conclusion

I am always a bit cautious about companies that need to grow at a high pace for many years to be fairly valued, as there is always the risk of growth rates suddenly slowing down and leaving investors with an extremely overvalued stock.

But in the case of Intuit, we have little hints that the business is really in trouble. Margins are improving again, return on invested capital is about 16% right now and also improving, and Intuit is still growing at a high pace—demonstrated by the last quarterly results. Additionally, it might be rather difficult to train GenAI applications on tax and accounting data, as such data is usually not publicly available, making it difficult for competitors to launch high-quality applications and really be a threat to Intuit. Overall, I see Intuit as a "Buy" for the first time in seven years.

data.title: GBP/USD Chart Alert: Bull Flag Pattern In Play Ahead Of Retail Sales Data
data.description:
By Zain Vawda

GBP/USD edged its way lower on Thursday as hopes of a ceasefire in the Middle East came under strain. Mixed reports and comments from both sides saw markets adopt a cautious approach, with the USD gaining a bid as a result.

Late in the day President Trump announced the delay of Iran's energy plant destruction by ten days, until April 6 at 08:00 PM Eastern Time. President Trump emphasized that talks between Washington and Tehran are going "very well" and he decided to pause at the request of the Iranian Government. Trump’s previous deadline was Friday, with the question now being whether this is genuine or another ruse ahead of the weekend?

Markets may remain concerned that the US could mount a ‘sneak attack’ over the weekend with defensive positioning, and haven demand likely to catch a bid as a result. Such a scenario could weigh on GBP/USD.

What do the technicals say?

Looking at the technical picture and on the H1 chart below, GBP/USD has edged its way lower since printing a fresh high around 1.34800 on March 23.

There is a bull flag pattern in play on the H1 chart with a breakout leading to a potential 100-pip rally to the upside. Such a move does face challenges though, as price is currently trading near 1.3333, sitting just below the 200-period Simple Moving Average (SMA).

There is also the 100 SMA (blue), which is currently above the 200 SMA (Black) at around the 1.3372 handle. Both of these MAs will need to be cleared first if a rally higher and breakout of the bull flag pattern are to materialize.

If the price remains below the 200 SMA (black line) and breaks the 1.3320 support, expect a move toward the YTD low of 1.3223.

GBP/USD Daily Chart, March 26, 2026

UK retail sales to serve as a catalyst?

Early on Friday, UK retail sales data is due in what is otherwise a rather quiet day on the economic calendar front. Market consensus is for a -0.8% MoM print, with the YoY print at 2.1%.

A better-than-expected figure may provide a temporary bounce for GBP/USD but is unlikely to inspire a sustained break of the bull flag pattern. Any gains may prove temporary without a material change to overall sentiment, which continues to support the greenback.

data.title: Mining M&A In 2025 - Copper, Gold Remain Center Stage
data.description:
S&P Global Energy tracks metals and mining M&A deals with a value of at least $10 million that involve 1 million ounces of gold or 100,000 metric tons of base metal in acquired reserves and resources (R&R). This report categorizes acquisition assets by primary metal — gold, copper, nickel and zinc — and does not split the acquisition cost for projects that contain more than one metal. Also included are project and company deals with lithium oxide, or Li2O (referred to as lithium in this article), in R&R, announced between Nov. 1, 2024, and Dec. 31, 2025. Terminated, non-equity deals — such as royalty and streaming — and "earn-in" transactions are not included in the analysis. The deal status is as of the time the data was compiled.

The take

The commodity preference of mining M&A activity flipped in favor of base metals in 2025, buttressed by a $27.96 billion megamerger between Anglo American PLC (AAUKF)(NGLOY) and Teck Resources Ltd. (TECK). The total deal value reached $52.71 billion across 50 deals, with gold deal value reaching a 15-year high. Lithium deal value was down 89% across just four deals amid price headwinds. A continued focus on diversified portfolios and consolidation is likely in 2026, and M&A activity may slow as the Middle East war continues.

High-priced and midsized deals bolstered M&A activity within the base metals and gold mining sector in 2025, with the aggregate deal value doubling year-over-year to $52.7 billion across 50 qualifying transactions. This marked the highest annual deal value in 18 years. Gold-focused acquisitions dominated in terms of deal count, representing 64% of all transactions. However, base metal deals accounted for more than half of the total deal value due to a megamerger. In contrast, 2024 had no megadeals or transactions above the $5 billion mark.

While there was a surge in deal value in 2025, due largely to the proposed merger between Anglo American and Teck Resources announced in September 2025, the overall number of deals fell 19% year-over-year. This dynamic drove a significant increase in average annual deal value, which reached $1.05 billion.

Nine deals valued at over $1 billion were announced during the year, with the five largest being direct company acquisitions. Company acquisitions accounted for the majority of overall deal value during the year, narrowly surpassing mining property transactions. The next-largest deal behind the Anglo-Teck merger was the $6.88 billion acquisition of New Gold Inc. by Coeur Mining Inc. (CDE) announced in November 2025. This was followed by Gold Fields Ltd.'s (GFI) purchase of Gold Road Resources Ltd. (ELKMF) for $3.69 billion.

The most significant property deal was Carcetti Capital Corp.'s $1.1 billion acquisition of the Hemlo gold (TPNEF) mine in Canada. At the commodity level, gold-focused acquisitions still dominated the period, with 32 deals amounting to a total value of $21.2 billion — the highest total recorded for gold since 2010.

The 14 copper-focused deals had a total deal value of $30.81 billion, with the Anglo-Teck merger constituting 91% of the total. Notably, the volume of acquired copper in R&R soared 200% from 2024, reaching 62.8 million mt. Nickel and zinc deals made up a smaller portion of overall M&A activity for the year, with three qualifying nickel deals collectively valued at $683.0 million and only one qualifying zinc deal priced at $10.7 million.

Anglo-Teck lifts base metal deals

As in 2024, copper dealmaking ramped up in the second half of the year, which saw 60% of the copper deal announcements. Companies classified as "Other" were the most prominent buyer type for the commodity, followed by majors, which were the buyers in four deals. The Anglo-Teck megamerger skewed the annual average copper deal value to $2.20 billion. Nearly all of the other copper deals were priced below $1 billion and split almost evenly between deals targeting companies and single mining properties. Excluding the megamerger, the annual average copper deal value would have dropped roughly 50% year-over-year. This trend toward smaller deals was expected, as most tier 1 copper assets are held by major producers, encouraging smaller buyers to pursue earlier-stage assets to build long-term optionality. In addition, copper prices saw large price swings during the year, driven by supply disruptions and trade policy changes, further encouraging brownfield acquisitions.

The year's defining deal was the proposed Anglo-Teck merger valued at $27.96 billion. Upon completion, the copper-focused deal will be the largest in the mining space in more than a decade, following Glencore PLC's (GLCNF) merger with Xstrata PLC in 2013. The deal exemplified buyers' continued pursuit of diversified copper-bearing portfolios, given the anticipated structural constraints affecting copper supply amid expectations of accelerating demand driven by electrification. The merger of equals is expected to create a top-tier global copper producer, with its largest assets located mainly in Chile, Peru and Canada. Over 51.7 million mt of copper in R&R changed hands, accounting for 83% of the year's total acquired R&R. Teck contributes a portfolio anchored by long-life copper assets, while Anglo American brings operational scale and geographic overlap in the Americas.

Copper's key role in the energy transition continued to attract producers seeking diversified portfolios. Increasingly, mining companies are broadening their operations to include copper mines to capitalize on the anticipated long-term upside. The second-largest copper deal was Harmony Gold Mining Co. Ltd.'s (HMY)(HGMCF) acquisition of MAC Copper Ltd. for $1.02 billion, which closed in October 2025. Harmony Gold — historically one of South Africa's largest gold producers — framed the transaction as a step toward becoming a global gold and copper producer, rather than a pure-play gold miner. Harmony Gold gained immediate copper exposure through the producing, high-grade CSA (Cobar) copper mine in Australia — MAC Copper's sole operating asset — without the long lead times typically associated with greenfield or early-stage copper mines.

Unlike copper, nickel remained in structural surplus in 2025, due primarily to rapid production growth in Indonesia, which has continued to encourage opportunistic and counter-cyclical acquisitions. Nickel deal activity comprised three qualifying transactions for over 8 million mt of nickel in R&R, with a total value of $683.0 million — a substantial increase from 2024's $20.3 million. The largest nickel-centric deal was MMG Ltd.'s (MMLTF) acquisition of Anglo American's nickel assets in Brazil for $500.0 million, announced in February 2025. According to Anglo American, divestment of the assets was a deliberate move to tighten focus on its copper, premium iron ore and crop nutrients businesses. The purchase of the Jacare and Morro Sem Bone mines marks MMG's first entry into Brazil and adds nickel to its base metals-focused portfolio.

Conversely, activity in the zinc sector remained muted, with only one qualifying transaction valued at $10.7 million. Cerrado Gold Inc.'s (CRDOF) acquisition of a 91% stake in Ascendant Resources Inc. included the Lagoa Salgada polymetallic project in Portugal, a zinc-dominant volcanogenic massive sulfide (VMS) project hosting zinc, copper, silver, gold, lead and barite. The acquisition of the project diversified Cerrado's primarily gold portfolio through base metals exposure, adding 302,723 mt of zinc in R&R. Outside of that transaction, a copper-focused deal — Vizsla Copper Corp.'s (VCUFF) purchase of Constantine Metal Resources Ltd. and the Palmer VMS project in Alaska for $42.0 million — also involved substantial zinc reserves.

Gold dealmaking reaches record high

With gold prices reaching new highs in 2025 amid geopolitical uncertainty, interest in the yellow metal was steadfast, even as investors rallied in pursuit of critical minerals. Mid-tier consolidation continued to characterize company takeovers, while development assets in stable jurisdictions remained attractive to buyers looking to secure future production.

Despite the 26% year-over-year drop in the number of deals to 32, total deal value grew 10%, and over 162.1 million ounces of gold in R&R were acquired. Majors accounted for 34% of the buyers, spending $16.3 million and favoring corporate buyouts and late-stage assets.

Building on momentum from the previous year, Canadian gold assets attracted $10.59 billion in investments across all buyer types, followed by assets in Australia. The largest gold transaction was Coeur Mining Inc.'s pending acquisition of New Gold Inc. for $6.88 billion, aimed at scaling operations and establishing a significant North American precious metals producer with assets across Canada, the US and Mexico. The second-largest deal saw Gold Fields Ltd. acquire Gold Road Resources Ltd. for $3.69 billion, consolidating its remaining 50% ownership in the Gruyere JV gold mine in Western Australia. In a similar vein, Equinox Gold Corp.'s (EQX) purchase of Calibre Mining Corp. for $2.13 billion was a consolidation move with scale in mind, creating an Americas-focused diversified gold producer with a suite of operations, led by Equinox's Greenstone and Calibre's Valentine Lake in Canada.

Price weakness shapes lithium deals

Following our previous study, lithium dealmaking slowed between November 2024 and December 2025, with only seven deals announced during that period, as a weak lithium price environment prevailed. Combined deal value for lithium reached $1.06 billion, while lithium in acquired R&R totaled over 4.1 million mt.

The four deals announced in 2025 accounted for 75% of the total value across the 13-month period, the largest of which is the $765.0 million partnership between POSCO Holdings Inc. (PKX) and Mineral Resources Ltd. (MinRes) (MALRF)(MALRY) announced in November 2025. Through the newly formed and incorporated joint venture established by MinRes, POSCO Holdings will acquire a total of 30% interest in the Wodgina and Mount Marion hard-rock lithium assets in Australia.

In August 2025, Sayona Mining Ltd. closed its acquisition of Piedmont Lithium Inc. (PLLTL), consolidating ownership of the North American Lithium (NAL) mine in Quebec, Canada, which had been jointly owned by the two companies. The unified ownership of NAL by the newly created Elevra Lithium Ltd. (ELVR) is expected to contribute to cost reduction and operational efficiency.

Outside of the announced deals, Rio Tinto Group (RIO) closed its acquisition of Arcadium Lithium PLC in March 2025, marking the second-largest lithium-focused acquisition in history and signaling cash-rich buyers' willingness to buy scale at the bottom of the lithium price cycle. With the late-2025 price rebound being seen as less of a recovery and more of a rebalancing, dealmaking in the lithium space will likely remain selective, targeting either tier 1 assets or low-cost operations.

More diversified portfolios incoming?

Companies are increasingly reshaping their portfolios to gain exposure to multiple commodities and capture upsides in an environment where organic growth has become challenging to achieve. Another growing trend that could persist into 2026 is producers diversifying through assets with scalable, polymetallic deposits to capitalize on multiple revenue streams. A recent example is Eldorado Gold Corp.'s (EGO) acquisition of Foran Mining Corp. (FMCXF), announced in February 2026, targeting the multicommodity McIlvenna Bay project in Canada.

The ongoing war in the Middle East could slow activity for copper, nickel and zinc. However, long-term electrification and decarbonization goals remain structurally important demand drivers through the next decade, and copper is expected to sit at the forefront of dealmaking. Supply constraints, geopolitical risks and declining ore grades, which have driven a good number of copper deals in the past two years, will continue to temper enthusiasm for high-cost acquisitions, particularly for smaller players. Strategic megamergers could still come into play, however. Conversely, gold's supportive price environment will continue to fuel deal activity, though declining grades and discoveries could mean more consolidation-driven transactions.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: Balchem: An Attractive Valuation Makes This Stock A Buy
data.description:
Investment Thesis

Balchem Corporation recently posted a strong quarterly earnings report with solid growth throughout the company. The company consistently rewards shareholders with above-average dividend growth, which is easily covered by a sub-20% payout ratio. Finally, the free cash flow per share grew by almost 50% in 2025, and relative to its historical FCF valuation, the stock appears to be trading for a discount, resulting in a BUY rating.

Previous Coverage

I originally covered Balchem Corporation (BCPC) in the summer of the last year, just prior to their Q2 earnings report. At that time I rated the stock a HOLD, despite decent financials and a growing dividend, because the forward return potential wasn't as attractive as I'd like it to be. Since my initial write-up, the stock has had its ups and downs but overall has remained quite flat. The stock has earned a small gain of less than 2%, underperforming the broad market, as measured by SPY, by a couple of percentage points.

About a month ago, Balchem Corp. announced their Q4 earnings report, so I thought now would be a good time to revisit the stock to see where it might go from here and provide an updated fair value based on new free cash flow numbers.

Q4 Earnings and Looking Ahead

On Friday, February 20th, Balchem Corporation announced their Q4 earnings with strong results. Non-GAAP earnings per share exceeded analyst estimates by $0.03, coming in at $1.31, on revenue of $263.6M, also slightly above consensus expectations, and a year-over-year increase of close to 10%.

We'll take a look at the earnings by segment, starting with the Human Nutrition and Health segment, which earned revenue of $166M (~64% of sales), a solid 12.7% increase compared to the same quarter a year ago. This growth stemmed from higher demand and a favorable product mix but was partially mitigated by a rise in operating expenses, as well as higher raw materials.

The Animal Nutrition and Health segment posted an increase of $2.8M, or about 5%, with total revenue just north of $61M (~22% of sales). Strong demand for farm animal food and supplements was the main reason for this increase. Furthermore, EBIT earnings climbed almost 9%, to close out the quarter at $36.8M, for the same reasons as the Human segment results.

Finally, the Specialty Products segment earned revenue of $34.8M (~14% of sales), an increase of $2M, or 6%, relative to Q4 2024, thanks to increased demand in the performance gases business unit. Additionally, EBIT earnings increased by a similar figure as the segment revenue, growing by a half million to finish the quarter at $10.5M.

Overall the growth was steady but was clearly driven by increased demand for their products, which is a positive, and even better that demand outpaced rising operating expenses and input costs.

As a dividend-focused investor, I always like to monitor this area of a business too, and BCPC doesn't disappoint. Late last year the company raised its dividend for the 17th consecutive year, announcing a 10.3% hike, and it is easily covered by a payout ratio that has only eclipsed 20% once over the past decade. And lastly, for the duration the company has paid a dividend, the annual growth has always been 10% or more, an incredible achievement.

In the week following the earnings release, which can be read/listened to here, shares of the stock rose about 6.5%. However, since that initial jump, the stock has struggled mightily and is down more than 7% overall since the earnings report.

Looking ahead to the company's next earnings call, analysts are projecting revenue of about $266.5M, which equates to growth of a little more than 6%. While normalized EPS is estimated to be $1.33, resulting in YoY growth of just over 9%. It's also worth mentioning that both analysts who cover Balchem have raised their earnings per share estimates during the last 90 days.

Risks/Headwinds

Despite a strong quarter for Balchem, the company has some risks they will need to face in order to maintain their success.

The first headwind BCPC has to deal with is higher raw material costs, which relates specifically to the Animal Nutrition segment, as the ingredients used here have been unstable. In the most recent earnings call, management mentioned the gross margin contracted about 40 bps in the quarter as a result of these increased costs. And unfortunately, this isn't the first time management has stated this concern, as it's been noted in multiple earnings calls over the past year.

The other headwind is an obvious one for most companies with global operations, but the uncertainty surrounding the tariff situation is far from over. This is especially true because soon after the Supreme Court struck down the tariffs, President Trump replaced them via another avenue, just at a different rate, and likely has more options he'll utilize if necessary. Smartly, Balchem does operate through various supply chains, which does limit some of its exposure, but nonetheless these tariffs are something they'll need to monitor in 2026 and possibly beyond.

Valuation

The last consideration is to determine if BCPC is trading for an attractive valuation. I explained my thought process and how to read the chart below in my first write-up, which can be read here.

In my initial article about BCPC, the stock was trading for about $161, very close to its estimated fair value. Currently, my free cash flow valuation model indicates that the stock is trading for a good-sized discount, as the free cash flow valuation zone rose a healthy amount in 2025, giving it a potential fair value of $215, implying an approximate 22% discount.

The company has a robust forward return potential (over the next 3-5 years) of just under 14%, with the components of that estimate being a forward dividend yield of 0.57%, a return to fair value factor of 4.06%, and an expected earnings growth rate of 9%.

Final Thoughts

In summary, Balchem's stock price has been pretty mute since my coverage last summer, essentially matching the return of the overall U.S. equity market. The company turned in an impressive quarterly earnings report with strong growth across each of the company's segments. Additionally, BCPC pays an annual dividend, with an outstanding dividend CAGR in excess of 10%, supported by a well-covered payout ratio. Ultimately, even though there are some headwinds they'll need to manage, the company appears to be trading for an attractive valuation while also offering a strong estimated forward return, which leads me to rate the stock a 'Buy' at its current levels.

data.title: Tracking Philippe Laffont's $40B Coatue Management Portfolio - Q4 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to Coatue Management's 13F stock portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on Coatue's regulatory 13F form filed on 02/17/2026. The 13F portfolio value decreased from $40.79B to $39.96B this quarter. Recent 13F reports have shown around 75 individual stock positions in the portfolio. The largest five stakes are Alphabet, Taiwan Semi, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Amazon. They add up to ~32% of the entire portfolio. Please visit our Tracking Philippe Laffont's Coatue Portfolio series to get an idea of their investment philosophy and our previous update for the fund's moves during Q3 2025.

Philippe Laffont was a telecom analyst at Tiger Management from 1996. He founded Coatue Management as a tech-focused long-short hedge fund in 1999 and is one of the most successful among the "tiger cubs". To know more about Julian Robertson and his legendary Tiger Management, check out Julian Robertson: A Tiger in the Land of Bulls and Bears.

Note: In May, Coatue Management launched Coatue Innovative Strategies Fund (CTEK), a closed-end fund with a $50K minimum and access only to accredited investors.

Stake Disposals

Alibaba Group Holding (BABA): The BABA stake was built during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$80 and ~$148. The next quarter saw the position sold down by ~80% at prices between ~$96 and ~$135. There was a ~130% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between ~$104 and ~$182. The disposal this quarter was at prices between ~$146 and ~$193. The stock currently trades at ~$125.

CoreWeave (CRWV): CRWV had an IPO in March. Shares started trading at ~$40 and currently go for $80.45. Coatue's position goes back to a Series C funding round in May 2024. There was a ~25% stake increase during Q2 2025 at prices between ~$33.50 and ~$187. The last quarter saw ~63% selling at prices between ~$84 and ~$166. The elimination this quarter was at prices between ~$64 and ~$153.

KKR & Co. (KKR): The 0.71% KKR stake was established during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$104 and ~$133. There was a ~20% stake increase during Q1 2025 at prices between ~$105 and ~$170. The last quarter saw a ~40% reduction in prices between ~$127 and ~$154. The position was sold this quarter at prices between ~$112 and ~$145. The stock is now at ~$90.

Atlassian Corp. (TEAM), Hinge Health (HNGE), and Intuit Inc. (INTU): These stakes were dropped during the quarter.

Stake Increases

Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM): The 6.56% TSM position was built during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$99 and ~$149. The next quarter saw a ~10% stake increase at prices between ~$127 and ~$180. There was ~18% selling during Q3 2024 at prices between ~$147 and ~$190. Q1 2025 saw a ~20% reduction at prices between ~$160 and ~$226. The stock is now at ~$326. There was a minor ~5% stake increase this quarter.

data.title: Lithium Junior Miners News For The Month Of March 2026
data.description:
Welcome to the March 2026 edition of the 'junior' lithium miner news. We have categorized those lithium miners that are not in commercial production as the juniors.

Note: Investors are reminded that many of the lithium juniors will most likely not be needed until the late 2020's (& 2030's) to supply the potentially booming electric vehicle [EV] and battery energy stationary storage [BESS] markets. This means investing in these companies requires a higher risk tolerance and a longer time frame.

The past month China lithium carbonate spot prices and spodumene prices were generally flat.

Lithium price news

Metal.com reported a Australian spodumene concentrate 6% Li2O CIF China spot price of USD 2,080, as of March 24, 2026, roughly flat the past month.

The China lithium carbonate spot price was flat last month, now at CNY 152,500.

China lithium carbonate spot price 5 year chart - CNY 152,500 (~USD 22,096) (source)

Katusa Research is forecasting a small lithium deficit to begin in 2026 (as of January 2026) (source)

RMIS forecasts lithium deficits to start after 2028, may grow very large without major new investments (as of May 2025) (source)

GGII, a Shenzhen-based consultancy, forecasts a balanced lithium market this year, a 10,000t LCE deficit in 2026, and a 60,000t deficit in 2027 (as of mid 2025) (source)

Lithium market news

For a summary of the latest lithium market news and the 'major' lithium company's news, investors can read: "Lithium Miners News For The Month Of March 2026" article. Highlights include:

  • Lithium stocks rise as Zimbabwe bans exports of lithium concentrates.
  • New Chinese breakthrough in lithium battery technology enables 700 Wh/kg energy density, using fluorinated hydrocarbon solvents.
  • UK enters commercial lithium production with geothermal plant launch in Cornwall, initial production is set at 100 tons per year.
  • Bloomberg: Lithium recovery has legs as demand outlook improves, SQM says.
  • Fastmarkets: Tightening supply expectations combined with macroeconomic uncertainty causes volatility. A tighter lithium market in early 2026, with demand outpacing supply.
  • CATL's 2025 net income climbed 42% YoT, to 72.2 billion yuan ($10.4B).
  • LG says it will supply Tesla with $4.3B of LFP batteries for BESS.
  • Albemarle forecasts global energy storage system production to grow 25 to 60% year over year in 2026.

Junior lithium miners company news

Galan Lithium [ASX:GLN] (GLNLF)

Galan is developing their flagship Hombre Muerto West ("HMW") Lithium Project located on the west side edge of the high grade, low impurity Hombre Muerto Salar in Argentina.

On March 2 Galan Lithium announced:

HMW Phase 1 construction update - 92% complete...

  • Nanofiltration plant assembly progressing to plan.
  • Construction expected to complete in the short term, transitioning to testing and commissioning.
  • Nanofiltration plant commissioning with raw brine expected to follow successful testing.
  • Significant brine inventory ready for processing.
  • First lithium chloride concentrate in H1 2026, first shipment H2 2026.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • H1, 2026 - Commence production and then ramp to 5.2ktpa LCE of lithium chloride production. Phase 2 to follow and ramp to 20Ktpa LCE.

Galan Lithium HMW site showing the nanofiltration plant (source)

LibertyStream Infrastructure Partners Inc. [TSXV:LIB]

On March 12 LibertyStream announced:

LibertyStream begins installation and integration of DLE extraction and refining systems at Select Water Solutions’ site; Initial production expected early Q2...this initial deployment is intended to support continued bulk sample production and customer qualification while establishing the systems-integration template for planned scaling at the site and future deployments across major U.S. basins, including the Permian and Bakken.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2026 - Start of small scale commercial production from Permian Basin oilfield brine.
  • 2027 - Targeting commercial volumes beginning in 2027.

Atlas Lithium Corp. (ATLX)

No news for the month.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2026 - Production targeted to begin at the Neves Lithium Project (a part of the Minas Gerais Lithium Project) in Brazil.

Standard Lithium [TSXV:SLI] (SLI)

On March 9 Standard Lithium announced:

Smackover Lithium signs first binding customer offtake agreement for the South West Arkansas Project...with Trafigura Trading LLC...Under the terms of this binding take-or-pay offtake agreement (the “Agreement”), the JV will supply Trafigura with 8,000 metric tonnes per year of battery-quality lithium carbonate over a 10-year period, beginning at the start of commercial production.

Lithium Ionic Corp. [TSXV:LTH] (OTCQB:LTHCF)

On March 25 Lithium Ionic announced:

Lithium Ionic secures offtake agreements with leading integrated lithium producers, including one of the world’s largest lithium hydroxide producers, for Bandeira Project production.

  • Five-year agreements with US$1,000t floor price, no discount to spot pricing and full upside exposure to support Project financing.
  • Lithium Ionic will supply a combined 170,000 tonnes per annum of spodumene concentrate under five-year binding take-or-pay offtake agreements to Yahua Group and Grand Chen, two integrated counterparties serving tier-one battery and EV manufacturers, including BYD and other leading OEMs..
  • US$20 million combined pre-payment facility associated with the offtake agreements, aligned with the Project’s advancement toward construction.

Lithium Americas [TSX:LAC] (LAC)

Lithium Americas has the Thacker Pass JV Project (LAC 62%, GM 38%, the U.S. DoE also holds a 5% Project stake via warrants) and ~5.2% equity in GT1.

On March 19 Lithium Americas announced:

Full Year 2025 results... 2025 marked a transformative year for Thacker Pass. Construction is advancing at full pace, and we are carrying that strong momentum into 2026... “Construction at Thacker Pass is progressing rapidly"...We remain on track for mechanical completion of Phase 1 in late 2027.

Upcoming catalysts:

  • 2026 - Thacker Pass Phase 1 construction to progress.
  • Late 2027 - Phase 1 (40,000tpa LCE) production from Thacker Pass Nevada (plan to eventually ramp to 160,000tpa in four phases of 40,000 tpa each at 4 year intervals starting from late 2027).

Vulcan Energy Resources [ASX: VUL] (OTCPK:VULNF)

Vulcan Energy Resources state that they have "the largest lithium resource in Europe" with a total of 15.85mt LCE, at an average lithium grade of 181 mg/L. The Company is in the development stage developing a geothermal lithium brine operation (geothermal energy plus lithium extraction plants) in the Upper Rhine Valley of Germany.

On March 17 Vulcan Energy Resources announced:

Vulcan secures lithium production licence for Lionheart...which is now funded and in construction. It is the first such licence to be granted in the Upper Rhine Valley Brine Field (URVBF), and in the state of Rhineland-Palatinate. Lionheart involves the construction of an integrated lithium and renewable energy project targeting production capacity of 24,000 tonnes of lithium hydroxide monohydrate (LHM), enough for ca. 500,000 electric vehicle batteries per annum, with a co-product of 275 GWh of renewable power and 560 GWh of renewable heat per annum for local consumers, over an estimated 30-year project life.

Upcoming catalysts include:

  • 2028 - Target to commence commercial production of 24,000tpa LiOH monohydrate in Phase One of the Lionheart Project in Germany, then ramp to 40,000tpa.

Atlantic Lithium Limited [LSE:ALL] [ASX:A11] (OTCQX:ALLIF)

Atlantic Lithium is working to progress its Ewoyaa JV Project in Ghana towards production.

On March 17 Atlantic Lithium announced:

Atlantic Lithium to raise up to c. US$16.4m through a Strategic Investment from Ghanaian investors and a third placement with Long State Investments Ltd.

On March 20 Atlantic Lithium announced:

Parliamentary Ratification of Ewoyaa Mining Lease. Parliament of Ghana issues official approval of the Mining Lease in respect of the Ewoyaa Lithium Project, representing a significant de-risking milestone...Under the terms of the Mining Lease, which was initially granted by Ghana's Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources in October 2023, Atlantic Lithium has the exclusive rights to carry out mining and commercial production activities over the Mining Lease area for an initial 15-year period, renewable in accordance with Ghanaian legislation...The revised terms comprise the alignment of the Project's royalty rate and Growth and Sustainability Levy to current legislated rates in Ghana.

Ghana royalties scale (source)

Frontier Lithium [TSXV:FL] (OTCQX:LITOF)

Frontier Lithium own the PAK Lithium (Spodumene) Project comprising 26,774 hectares and located 175 kilometers north of Red Lake in northwestern Ontario. The PAK deposit is a lithium-cesium-tantalum [LCT] type pegmatite containing high-purity, technical-grade spodumene (below 0.1% iron oxide). JV with Mitsubishi Corporation to advance the Project signed in 2024.

On March 2 Frontier Lithium announced:

Frontier Lithium signs memorandum of understanding with Panasonic Energy to advance north American battery supply chain. Under the MoU, Panasonic Energy has expressed interest in procuring lithium hydroxide from the PAK Lithium Project. The parties also intend to explore broader collaboration opportunities to advance a resilient and sustainable battery supply chain in North America, including potential technical cooperation. The parties to the MoU have confirmed their willingness to negotiate a definitive offtake agreement at an appropriate stage of the Project.

On March 9 Frontier Lithium announced:

Frontier Lithium recognized under G7 critical minerals production alliance and federal permitting coordination and grants stock options.

Savannah Resources [LSE:SAV] [GR:SAV] (OTCPK:SAVNF)

On March 23 Savannah Resources announced:

Project Timetable Update. DFS and RECAPE on track for summer 2026 completion...reiterates the goal of first production in 2028.

ioneer Ltd [ASX:INR] [GR:4G1] (IONR)

ioneer Ltd. 100% owns its flagship Rhyolite Ridge Lithium-Boron Project in Nevada, USA.

On March 18 ioneer announced:

Annual Report to shareholders for the six months ended 31 December 2025...Our achievements during this reporting period demonstrate Ioneer’s continued focus on advancing the strategic partnering process and progressing Rhyolite Ridge towards making a Final Investment Decision [FID]...We continue to advance project-related work following the closing of a $996 million loan from the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Energy Dominance Financing in January 2025.

Critical Elements Lithium Corp. [TSXV:CRE] [GR:F12] (OTCQX:CRECF)

No news for the month.

Critical Metals Corp (CRML) ("CRML") - Majority shareholder is European Lithium Ltd [ASX:EUR] (OTCQB:EULIF)

CRML owns the "fully licensed" Wolfsberg Lithium Project in Austria. They also own the Tanbreez Rare Earth Project in Greenland.

On March 16 European Lithium announced: "31 December 2025 Half Year Report."

Winsome Resources Limited [ASX:WR1] [FSE:4XJ] (OTCQB:WRSLF) - Takeover planned by Li-FT Power - likely to finalize in May 2026

On March 26 Winsome Resources announced:

Court approves convening of Scheme Meetings and distribution of Scheme Booklet...The Scheme Meetings will be held in person on 5 May 2026.

PMET Resources Inc. [TSX:PMET][ASX:PMT] (OTCQX:PMETF) - formerly Patriot Battery Metals

PMET Resources own the Shaakichiuwaanaan Project (formerly named the Corvette Lithium Project) in James Bay, Quebec.

On March 18 PMET Resources announced:

Widest Caesium drill intercept to date at the Helios Caesium Zone in final batch of 2025 drill results from Shaakichiuwaanaan...4.9 m at 3.65% Cs2O.

Global Lithium Resources [ASX:GL1] (OTCPK:GBLRF)

On March 3 Global Lithium Resources announced:

Manna Lithium Project continues to advance with submission of MDCP and key project appointments...the Mining Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP) for GL1’s Manna Lithium Project has been lodged with the Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration (DMPE) in Western Australia.

Delta Lithium [ASX:DLI] (formerly Red Dirt Metals)

On March 10 Delta Lithium announced:

Half Yearly Report and Accounts...During the half year ended 31 December 2025, Delta focussed on its two key projects in Western Australia. At Mt Ida, Delta successfully completed the demerger of its Mt Ida Gold Project into a new, standalone entity, named Ballard Mining Limited (Ballard).

European Metals Holdings [ASX:EMH] [AIM:EMH] [GR:E861] (OTCQX:EMHLF)(OTCQX:EMHXY)

No significant news for the month.

Lithium South Development Corp. [TSXV:LIS] (OTCQB:LISMF) - Share Purchase Agreement with POSCO Argentina (expected to finalize in March 2026)

Multiple 200+ metre intervals of continuous spodumene pegmatite and provides an update on the inaugural Mineral Resource estimate at the Cisco Lithium Project, Quebec, Canada...Drill hole CS25-071 encountered eight (8) spodumene pegmatite intervals, with the widest continuous interval of 264.6 m, followed by an additional 152.9 m of continuous spodumene pegmatite...The Company advises that completion of the Mineral Resource Estimate (“MRE”) has been extended to allow for additional geological modelling work...The Exploration Target estimated a range of potential lithium mineralization of 215 to 329 million tonnes at a grade ranging from 1.0 to 1.38% Li2O, based only on the first 40 holes drilled.

Conclusion

China lithium carbonate spot prices and spodumene spot prices were generally flat the past month.

Highlights for the month were:

  • Zimbabwe bans exports of lithium concentrates.
  • Fastmarkets: A tighter lithium market in early 2026, with demand outpacing supply.
  • Galan Lithium HMW Phase 1 construction 92% complete.
  • Lithium Ionic secures combined 170,000tpa spodumene binding offtake agreements with leading integrated lithium producers, Yahua Group and Grand Chen.
  • Lithium Americas Thacker Pass construction remains on track for mechanical completion of Phase 1 in late 2027.
  • Vulcan secures lithium production licence for Lionheart.
  • Atlantic Lithium Parliamentary Ratification of Ewoyaa Mining Lease (noting a sliding scale Ghana Gov. royalty system applies).
  • Frontier Lithium signs MOU with Panasonic Energy, may lead to a potential future offtake agreement.
  • Winsome Resources takeover by Li-FT Power - Court approves convening of Scheme Meetings and distribution of Scheme Booklet.
  • Q2 Metals drills interval of 264.6m, followed by an additional 152.9m of continuous spodumene pegmatite at their Cisco Lithium Project in Canada.
  • E3 Lithium receives conditional approval for up to C$36.5 million in Federal Government funding to accelerate its Clearwater Project.
  • Rock Tech and Siemens form Strategic Partnership to transfer proven German lithium conversion expertise to Canada’s next strategic lithium conversion project.
  • Neometals enters US Lithium‑Potash Brine JV.
